title: Rich Roasted Irish Rice Vermicelli Noodles
description: A fresh and molten Irish dish featuring rice vermicelli, perfect for a midnight craving, and diabetic-friendly. This recipe is a twist on traditional Irish cuisine with a focus on healthy ingredients.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and blanch the rice vermicelli for 3-5 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the garlic and onion until softened.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the salt, pepper, and grated cheddar until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Add the cooked rice vermicelli to the skillet and toss to combine with the sauce.
Serve the noodles hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
